headline: Blurred Vision? See an Eye Specialist in Chennai
subheadline: Blurred vision can have many causes -- Dr. Rajeswari will identify the real cause, not just guess.
metaDescription: Experiencing blurred vision? See Dr. Rajeswari in KK Nagar, Chennai for a thorough evaluation. Call or book online.

whyPatientsChoose.title: We Find the Real Cause
whyPatientsChoose.description: A comprehensive evaluation, not a quick glasses prescription, to identify what's actually going on.

symptoms: Gradual blurring over weeks or months
Sudden blurred vision in one or both eyes
Blurred vision with glare or halos at night

processSteps.title: Vision & Refraction Test
processSteps.description: Checking whether a simple glasses prescription is the cause.

processSteps.title: Eye Health Examination
processSteps.description: Ruling out cataracts, retina conditions, or other underlying issues.

processSteps.title: Clear Next Steps
processSteps.description: Whether it's glasses, treatment, or reassurance -- you'll know what's going on.

causeExploration.title: Possible Causes of Blurred Vision
causeExploration.causesIntro: Blurred vision is a symptom, not a diagnosis -- it's your eyes' way of signaling that something needs attention, but it doesn't tell you what on its own. In many cases, the cause is straightforward and treatable. The purpose of the examination is to identify the cause accurately and ensure nothing important is missed.
commonCauses: Refractive error (your glasses prescription has changed)
causeExploration.causesOutro: The same symptom can have many different causes. Identifying the cause accurately is the first step toward effective treatment.
causeExploration.urgentTitle: When Should You Seek Prompt Evaluation?
causeExploration.urgentIntro: Most blurred vision can wait for a scheduled appointment. But a few situations deserve prompt evaluation, ideally the same day:
urgentScenarios: Sudden blurred vision, especially in one eye
Vision loss or a sudden dark patch in one eye
Blurred vision along with eye pain
New flashes of light or floaters
New or unexplained blurred vision if you have diabetes
Blurring severe enough to affect daily activities like reading or driving

doctorsPerspective: Many patients assume blurred vision means they simply need new glasses. Sometimes that's true. Sometimes the cause is a cataract, diabetes-related changes, or another eye condition altogether. I don't guess based on symptoms alone -- the first step is always a proper examination to identify the actual reason, and only then do we talk about what, if anything, needs to be done.
